SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.allocation

Class SourceAllocator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.allocation.SourceAllocator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class SourceAllocator extends Object implements Serializable
High-level facade for linear recovery-factor production allocation.

Allocates the production of a process flowsheet back to its individual sources (wells, templates, commingled feeds) using frozen per-unit per-component split factors and a linear proxy network. It is a fast, scalable alternative to per-source component tagging: one rigorous base-case run with a single common component slate is enough, after which any number of sources can be allocated by superposition at a cost that scales with components × units, not with the number of sources.

The method works for any conservative separation / scrubber / column / valve / cooler / mixer / splitter network, including recycle and reflux loops, and therefore applies to all oil and gas field process configurations. Reactive or contacting units (amine, glycol, MEG, scavengers) are out of scope for strict mass conservation but are handled as black boxes using whatever redistribution the base case produced; for hydrocarbon allocation, MEG/water handling does not affect the result.

Typical usage: 

 SourceAllocator allocator = new SourceAllocator();
 allocator.setBaseCase(process); // process already run once
 allocator.addSource("Well-A", wellAFeed);
 allocator.addSource("Well-B", wellBFeed);
 allocator.addCustodyOutlet("ExportGas", exportGas, ProductType.GAS);
 allocator.addCustodyOutlet("ExportOil", exportOil, ProductType.OIL);
 allocator.addCustodyOutlet("ProducedWater", water, ProductType.WATER);

 ProductionAllocationResult result = allocator.allocate();
 double gasFromA = result.getProductAllocation("Well-A", ProductType.GAS, "kg/hr");
 String json = result.toJson();

If no sources or custody outlets are tagged explicitly, they are auto-detected from the flowsheet topology (external feeds become sources, terminal product streams become custody outlets with an inferred product type).

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team
See Also:

    addCustodyOutlet(String name, StreamInterface stream, ProductType productType)
    Tags a custody outlet by name, stream and product category.

    Tags a custody outlet.
    SourceAllocator

    Tags a production source by name and feed stream.

    Tags a production source.

    Runs the allocation: builds source injections, solves the linear network per component, maps node flows onto custody outlets, and optionally renormalises to base-case totals.
    private void
    autoDetectCustodyOutlets()
    Auto-detects custody outlets from terminal product streams, inferring each product type from the stream's dominant phase.
    private void
    autoDetectSources()
    Auto-detects sources from external feed streams (inlets not produced by any node).

    getBaseCase()
    Gets the base-case process system (available after setBaseCase(ProcessSystem)).

    Gets the list of tagged custody outlets in registration order.

    Gets the recovery-factor extractor (available after setBaseCase(ProcessSystem)).

    Gets the configurable linear solver.

    Gets the list of tagged production sources in registration order.

    Infers a product category for a stream from the dominant phase (by mole fraction) of its fluid.

    Sets the base-case process and extracts the frozen split factors.
    SourceAllocator
    setEnforceMassClosure(boolean enforceMassClosure)
    Sets whether the result is renormalised so each custody outlet's per-component totals exactly match the base-case (measured) values.

  • Constructor Details

    • SourceAllocator

      public SourceAllocator()

  • Method Details

    • setBaseCase

      public SourceAllocator setBaseCase(ProcessSystem process)
      Sets the base-case process and extracts the frozen split factors. The process must already have been run so that all stream compositions and flows are populated.
      Parameters:
      process - the base-case process system; must be non-null and already solved
      Returns:
      this allocator (fluent)

    • addSource

      public SourceAllocator addSource(String name, StreamInterface feedStream)
      Tags a production source by name and feed stream.
      Parameters:
      name - the source name; must be non-null and unique
      feedStream - the feed stream entering the process; must be non-null
      Returns:
      this allocator (fluent)

    • addSource

      public SourceAllocator addSource(AllocationSource source)
      Tags a production source.
      Parameters:
      source - the source to add; must be non-null
      Returns:
      this allocator (fluent)

    • addCustodyOutlet

      public SourceAllocator addCustodyOutlet(String name, StreamInterface stream, ProductType productType)
      Tags a custody outlet by name, stream and product category.
      Parameters:
      name - the outlet name; must be non-null and unique
      stream - the terminal product stream; must be non-null
      productType - the product category; must be non-null
      Returns:
      this allocator (fluent)

    • addCustodyOutlet

      public SourceAllocator addCustodyOutlet(CustodyOutlet outlet)
      Tags a custody outlet.
      Parameters:
      outlet - the custody outlet to add; must be non-null
      Returns:
      this allocator (fluent)

    • setEnforceMassClosure

      public SourceAllocator setEnforceMassClosure(boolean enforceMassClosure)
      Sets whether the result is renormalised so each custody outlet's per-component totals exactly match the base-case (measured) values. Enabled by default.
      Parameters:
      enforceMassClosure - true to enforce mass closure
      Returns:
      this allocator (fluent)

    • allocate

      public ProductionAllocationResult allocate()
      Runs the allocation: builds source injections, solves the linear network per component, maps node flows onto custody outlets, and optionally renormalises to base-case totals.
      Returns:
      the allocation result
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if setBaseCase(ProcessSystem) has not been called

    • autoDetectSources

      private void autoDetectSources()
      Auto-detects sources from external feed streams (inlets not produced by any node).

    • autoDetectCustodyOutlets

      private void autoDetectCustodyOutlets()
      Auto-detects custody outlets from terminal product streams, inferring each product type from the stream's dominant phase.

    • inferProductType

      static ProductType inferProductType(StreamInterface stream)
      Infers a product category for a stream from the dominant phase (by mole fraction) of its fluid.
      Parameters:
      stream - the stream to classify; must be non-null
      Returns:
      the inferred product type, or ProductType.UNKNOWN if classification fails

    • getExtractor

      public RecoveryFactorExtractor getExtractor()
      Gets the recovery-factor extractor (available after setBaseCase(ProcessSystem)).
      Returns:
      the extractor, or null if no base case is set

    • getNetwork

      public AllocationNetwork getNetwork()
      Gets the assembled proxy network (available after setBaseCase(ProcessSystem)).
      Returns:
      the network, or null if no base case is set

    • getBaseCase

      public ProcessSystem getBaseCase()
      Gets the base-case process system (available after setBaseCase(ProcessSystem)).
      Returns:
      the base-case process system, or null if no base case is set

    • getSolver

      public LinearAllocationSolver getSolver()
      Gets the configurable linear solver.
      Returns:
      the solver

    • getSources

      public List<AllocationSource> getSources()
      Gets the list of tagged production sources in registration order.

      Used by downstream tools such as AllocationUncertaintyEstimator that need to walk the same source list that allocate() uses. Auto-detected sources do not appear in this list until allocate() has been called.

      Returns:
      an unmodifiable view of the registered sources

    • getCustodyOutlets

      public List<CustodyOutlet> getCustodyOutlets()
      Gets the list of tagged custody outlets in registration order.

      Used by downstream tools such as AllocationUncertaintyEstimator that need to walk the same custody-outlet list that allocate() uses. Auto-detected outlets do not appear in this list until allocate() has been called.

      Returns:
      an unmodifiable view of the registered custody outlets
